metadata.title: Build Group vs Phase Velocity with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = envelope (group velocity); CRIMSON = carrier crests (phase velocity). INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=group vs phase simulation; B05=brand outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, wave packet dispersion. Quadratic dispersion omega equals h-bar k-squared over two m. Central wavevector k-zero equals one nanometer-inverse. Animate the wave packet. Show the carrier crests moving at phase velocity and the envelope moving at group velocity, which is twice the phase velocity. Three things: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  wave packet: ω=ℏk²/2m, k₀=1nm⁻¹
  v_phase = ω₀/k₀ = ℏk₀/2m
  v_group = dω/dk = ℏk₀/m = 2×v_phase
  animate envelope + carrier crests"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find these two lines: v-group equals h-bar times k-zero over m, and v-phase equals h-bar times k-zero over two-m. Check: the ratio v-group over v-phase equals exactly two. That is the key prediction for quadratic dispersion. Any other ratio means the dispersion relation is wrong. Thirty seconds.

props.code:
# wave_packet.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar = 1.0546e-34
m_e  = 9.109e-31
k0   = 1e9           # 1 nm^-1

omega0 = hbar * k0**2 / (2 * m_e)  # rad/s

# ← the physics: group and phase velocity
v_group = hbar * k0 / m_e  # ≈ 1.16e5 m/s
v_phase = hbar * k0 / (2 * m_e)  # = v_group/2

# ratio: v_group / v_phase = 2.000  (quadratic)
print(v_group / v_phase)  # 2.0

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the ratio. The group velocity is exactly twice the phase velocity. The envelope chip shows two-x. Second: the crest motion. Watch the carrier crests — they slide BACKWARD through the envelope as time advances. The crests move at half the speed of the envelope. That is the mismatch between phase and group velocity. Both checks pass. Ready to iterate.

Iterate: claude — update wave-packet dot py — change k-zero to two nanometers-inverse. The group velocity doubles. The phase velocity also doubles. The ratio stays at two — it is a property of the dispersion relation, not of the specific wavevector. One parameter, one confirmation, one re-run. The loop is the skill.

props.command:
claude "update wave_packet.py:
  → change k0 from 1 nm⁻¹ to 2 nm⁻¹
  → show v_group and v_phase both double
  → ratio v_group/v_phase stays at 2"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
